observation: I can see a long list of countries with flags and names displayed on the screen, including Indonesia which is visible. I also see language selection options at the bottom of the screen showing 'English' and other languages. There's a 'Sign up now' button at the bottom that appears to be the main call-to-action. Since I previously selected a country dropdown, I now need to select a specific country from this list to proceed with the registration.
reasoning: As an elderly user, I need to select a country from this comprehensive list. I can see Indonesia is prominently displayed and is a good choice since many financial platforms accept users from Indonesia. This will allow me to proceed with the registration flow. The text is large and clear enough for me to read.
ux_issues: Very long scrollable country list might be overwhelming for elderly users
No search functionality visible to quickly find a specific country

outcome.visual_observation: UX issues detected: Very long scrollable country list might be overwhelming for elderly users, No search functionality visible to quickly find a specific country
outcome.diagnosis: Analysis completed - see evidence for details

dual_diagnosis.visual_observation: The screen successfully transitioned from a country selection list view to a residence selection form. Indonesia is now pre-selected in a dropdown field, and the interface shows 'Select your residence' with explanatory text and a 'Next' button to continue the sign-up flow.
